generated_description: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.
name: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum)
summary: St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum) is a yellow-flowered herb with well-studied antidepressant effects for mild-to-moderate depression. Its active constituents (hypericin, hyperforin) inhibit reuptake of serotonin, dopamine, and norepinephrine. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy comparable to low-dose SSRIs for mild depression, with fewer side effects in that population.

generated_description: Turkey Tail (Trametes versicolor) is a shelf mushroom yielding two extensively studied polysaccharopeptides: PSK (krestin) and PSP. These compounds stimulate NK cells, macrophages, and T-cell activity, and PSK is approved in Japan as an adjunct cancer immunotherapy. Human evidence for standalone immune support in healthy populations is limited but supportive.
